subject: [PATCH v3 10/10] docs: fuse: add io-uring buffer pool and zero-copy documentation

2026-06-02.patch_summary: This patch adds documentation to the Linux kernel's FUSE (Filesystem in Userspace) implementation for using io-uring buffer pools and zero-copy techniques. The documentation explains how to use buffer pools, which decouple the relationship between entries and payload buffers, allowing for more optimal memory usage. It also describes how fuse manages buffer pools internally through a buffer ring, enabling features like incremental buffer consumption and multiple buffer sizes per ring. This patch aims to improve performance by reducing unnecessary copies of data between user space and kernel space.
